methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rhoman Aerospace is a seed-stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The comparable universe consists of seven private companies in this space, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Thoron. No public companies with direct exposure to this subsector were identified.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only four companies total that meet narrow peer criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier), falling short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for reliable median-based valuation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and capital requirements across drone and autonomous platforms.

Without a sufficiently dense peer set at matching stage and scale, this valuation cannot be supported by triangulated multiples-based methods. Subsequent analysis would require alternative approaches such as venture capital method modeling, precedent transaction analysis of seed-stage rounds in adjacent autonomous systems verticals, or implicit round-sizing frameworks tied to specific technical or commercial milestones. The analysis is constrained by data limitations rather than company-specific factors; prospective valuation work should either expand the comparable set or employ methods not dependent on peer multiples.
